mnemonics: CPU: ALU Brains, CU Traffic, Registers Quick Access
keyPoints: The Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) performs all calculations and logical operations.
The Control Unit (CU) directs the operation of the processor by interpreting instructions.
Registers provide the fastest form of memory access for the CPU to hold data currently being processed.
commonMistakes: Thinking the ALU fetches instructions (it's the CU's job).
Believing registers are a type of secondary storage (they are primary, volatile, and inside the CPU).
